intake.user_request: Bulletproof concept #6 — drop the two-grain debate. Pick file-grain (worst severity wins) and delete element-grain code paths from anything outside the analyzer. Most consumers don't need element resolution; ones that do can ask the analyzer directly.

classification.plan_type_rationale: Two-phase: Phase 1 is audit-only (inventory all element-grain aggregation paths outside the analyzer, produce audit-matrix.md). Phase 2 is deletion gated on Phase 1 hard stop. Scope is bounded but requires careful inventory before code removal.

in_scope: Audit all element-grain aggregation code paths outside src/analyzer/ and src/pipeline/analyzer.ts
Delete element-grain-to-file-grain aggregation in output-validator.ts (buildFileHeaderStatusMap iterates state.elements)
Replace with file-grain data sourced directly from graph.json (already has file-grain header status from pipeline Phase 5)
Confirm skip-count aggregation in indexing-orchestrator.ts is chunk-grain (already correct) — no change needed
tsc clean (both tsconfig.json and tsconfig.build.json) at close
AC-05a (2415===2415 element-grain) and AC-05b (263===263 file-grain) PASS at close
out_of_scope: Changes to skip-count/chunksSkipped paths (already chunk-grain — correct)
Changing the ValidationReport schema (11-field schema is locked)

constraints: DR-PHASE-7-B locked-additive: no new fields may be added without paired invariant test
ValidationReport 11-field schema is locked — output shape must not change
Phase 1 hard stop: audit-matrix.md must be committed before Phase 2 begins
Frozen-fixture dual-AC invariants must PASS: AC-05a 2415===2415, AC-05b 263===263
